sessions.intent: Design Data's variable→wire→variable round trip: flat shape, signing at channel, merge the two plang serializers, drop Envelope.

before.assumptions: Two plang serializers (application/plang and application/plang+data) duplicate STJ setup. The plang+data serializer builds a parallel Envelope class to bypass [JsonIgnore] on Signature. Stream.WriteCore strips the Data wrapper before handing to the serializer. Compress double-wraps (gzip Data inside archived Data). ISerializer accepts object?, but PLang only ever ships Data.
before.risk: Touches the channel write path and four serializer implementations atomically. Any caller that relied on the polymorphic object? input or on the data.Value strip will break — but per Ingi's assertion, those callers don't exist in PLang's actual flow because Data is the universal currency.

actions.context: Spine + wire-shape reference. Spine carries the design narrative and stage index; wire-shape carries concrete JSON examples for plain / compressed / encrypted / nested-value cases.

Four stage files. 1 tightens ISerializer to Data + unstrips Stream.WriteCore. 2 merges the plang serializers, drops Envelope, moves EnsureSigned to Channel. 3 flattens Compress. 4 vocabulary sweep — drop 'envelope' word.

Added 'Data Is Opaque to Its Consumers' principle under Core Philosophy. Captures the insight from this session: every layer in the pipeline handles Data as an opaque unit, only the encoder at the leaf walks properties.

after.status: Initial design plan complete. Four stages carved, wire-shape reference written, summary logged. Ready for test-designer to produce test-strategy.md and test-coverage.md, or for coder to begin Stage 1.
after.health: Design is settled. Conversation with Ingi covered the round-trip semantics, name as addressability coordinate, signing position, and the flat wire shape. No open architectural questions.
after.notes: Out of scope and explicitly parked: algorithm tagging on archived/encryption (the +variant pattern), protobuf encoder, Encrypt path flattening (waits for crypto service), inspection-path serialization (data.ToString for debug).

sessions.intent: Merge runtime2 into data-serialize-cleanup; reflect typed-action-returns in plan + Stage 1

before.assumptions: Plan and stages were carved against the pre-merge serializer surface. ISerializer returned bare T? and threw on parse errors. Stage 1 was going to tighten both return shape and input shape in one stroke.
before.risk: The merge could have changed the surface enough to invalidate other stages — needs an audit before claiming the plan still stands.

actions.context: Merged origin/runtime2 cleanly. Confirmed: Stages 2/3/4 unchanged; Stage 1 splits — return half done by typed-action-returns commit 5b1b894c4, input half remains.

plan.md problem #3 + ISerializer decision section + stage index annotated. Stage 1 rewritten to enumerate done-vs-remaining and narrow scope to input-tightening. Stage 2 code example updated to Task<Data> + try/catch. Summary gets a new top entry recording the merge and the changes.

after.status: Plan is reconciled with runtime2 surface. Stage 1 is narrower but still required (input side); other stages stand.
after.notes: Coder/test-designer pickup should read stage-1-iserializer-data.md fresh — the done-list at the top prevents re-doing the merged work.

sessions.intent: Lock in design pivots from review: sign-in-converter, canonicalization fix, Properties-on-wire (new Stage 4); rewrite plan + stages + wire-shape accordingly

before.assumptions: Plan said signing happens at the channel (outermost only); Properties were [JsonIgnore]; the type field in wire-shape used variable-name instead of schema-name; Stage 2 deliverable was "channel signs before WriteCore".
before.risk: The pivot to sign-in-converter touches Stage 2 and Stage 3 narratives at once. Properties-on-wire adds a real architectural stage (C# shape change + wire emission + variable-syntax change). Risk is leaving the stages inconsistent if any one of them keeps the old framing.

actions.context: Rewrote the Signing decision section (sign-if-missing during converter walk, three properties: forwarding, compress, list-of-Data). Added Properties cross-cutting decision (Dictionary<string,object?> primitives, flat top-level wire, %x.field% vs %x!key%, reserved keys, sign-walk-skips). Updated stages table to 5 stages. Updated test-surface bullets to match new behaviour.

Substantial rewrite. Dropped "channel signs before WriteCore" deliverable; replaced with sign-in-converter (wire converter calls EnsureSigned during walk). Added crypto/Default.cs canonicalization fix (hash through wire-direction options). Updated code example, design narrative, risks, and coder verification list.

Replaced the "no signing inside Compress" section (which was wrong) with the new framing: Compress routes through the registered serializer; the converter signs D1 during the byte-conversion automatically; this also fixes today's bug where _envelopeJsonOptions strips Signature from compressed bytes. Updated Properties round-trip note (will survive after Stage 4). Added sign-then-compress verification.

New Stage 4: Properties flatten to the wire. Scope, deliverables (Properties.cs C# rewrite, wire converter Write/Read, %!key% syntax, reserved-key enforcement), design rationale, risks, coder verification. Stage 5 is the renamed vocabulary sweep (was Stage 4).
Renamed to stage-5-vocabulary-sweep.md to make room for Stage 4 = Properties on wire. Vocabulary sweep is unchanged otherwise.

Fixed plain-Data example to show type:"object" for untyped (vs type:"user" for Data<User> in sibling example). Added LLM-response example showing Properties flattened to top-level (cost, model). Updated compressed-Data narrative to show inner Data carries its own signature (sign-during-byte-conversion). Updated nested-Data section to show inner Datas each have signatures, with canonicalization binding the chain. Updated "What's NOT on the wire" and round-trip invariants.
Added new top entry recording the three pivots (signing, canonicalization, Properties) and the new Stage 4. Stage status table updated to 5 stages.

after.status: Plan + 5 stages + wire-shape now consistent with all design decisions from the review session. Stage 1 is partial-complete (return half merged); Stages 2-5 are pending.
after.notes: Ready for coder pickup. Stage 2 carries the biggest surface (merge plang serializers + sign-in-converter + canonicalization). Stage 4 (Properties) is independent enough that it could run in parallel with Stage 3 once Stage 2 lands.
